content:
{"selectedIdeas":[{"title":"Model Showdown — Side-by-Side Battle Arena","description":"Let users run the same prompt against 2-3 AI models simultaneously and see responses, latency, token count, and rupee cost in real-time columns. Users vote which response 'won' and results feed a live public leaderboard of 'Best Model for X task in India'. Taps into the desi love of comparison shopping — like comparing specs on Flipkart before buying.","viralPotential":"Users screenshot and share their battle results on Twitter/LinkedIn with 'GPT-4o vs Claude vs Llama — which won for my use case?' — each share is organic marketing. The public leaderboard gives developers a reason to come back weekly as rankings shift.","mvpScope":"Static list of 3-4 pre-integrated models. Single prompt input box, 2-column response grid, hardcoded cost-per-token math for INR display. No auth needed for first 3 battles (then soft-gate). Store battle votes in a simple Postgres table. No streaming required — just parallel fetch + render.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Battle Setup: Two model selector dropdowns side by side, a shared prompt textarea in the center, and a big 'Start Battle ⚔️' CTA button with estimated max cost shown in ₹ before submitting","Screen 2 — Live Results Grid: Two cards rendering responses token-by-token with a live ₹ cost ticker below each, latency badge (e.g. '1.2s'), and a 'This one won 👍' thumb button per column","Screen 3 — Public Leaderboard: Sortable table of models ranked by win-rate per task category (Coding, Summarisation, Translation, Creative), filterable by date range, with a 'Challenge this ranking' CTA that pre-fills the Battle Setup"],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":null},{"title":""Ship in 60s" — One-Click API Snippet Generator","description":"After a user picks a model, they select their stack (Python, Node.js, cURL, Next.js API route) and instantly get a copy-paste ready code snippet pre-filled with the AI Bazaar endpoint, their masked API key, and a working example prompt. Includes a 'Test Live in Browser' button that actually fires the call and shows the response — zero setup, zero context-switching.","viralPotential":"Developers share the snippet on GitHub READMEs and Discord servers with 'Integrated in 60 seconds via AI Bazaar' — the platform name is baked into every shared snippet. Lowers the activation energy so dramatically that word-of-mouth spreads through dev communities like college coding clubs and startup WhatsApp groups.","mvpScope":"Static code templates per language stored as TS string literals. A language tab selector (4 tabs). API key injection via a simple string replace on the authenticated user's key (masked after first 4 chars). The 'Test Live' button calls an internal Next.js API route that proxies to the chosen model. No backend changes needed beyond one proxy route.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Snippet Panel (embedded in Model Detail page): Language tab strip (Python | Node | cURL | Next.js), syntax-highlighted code block with a floating 'Copy' button, and a yellow 'Test Live ▶' button below the snippet","Screen 2 — Live Test Drawer: Slides up from the bottom, shows an editable prompt input pre-filled with the example, a 'Run ▶' button, response output area with token count and ₹ cost, and a 'View Full Docs →' link"],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":null},{"title":"Bazaar Digest — Weekly AI Cost & Usage Report Card","description":"Every Monday morning, users get a beautifully designed shareable 'Report Card' — a single-page visual summary of last week's API usage: total ₹ spent, top model used, most expensive prompt category, cost trend vs prior week, and a personalised tip like 'Switch summarisation calls to Llama 3.1 and save ₹420/week'. Designed to look like an Instagram story card so people actually share it.","viralPotential":"Developers and founders love sharing 'I built X and it only cost me ₹340 in AI inference this week' on LinkedIn and Twitter. The card has a subtle AI Bazaar watermark + QR code — every share is a branded impression. Finance-conscious Indian startup culture makes cost bragging a genuine social behaviour.","mvpScope":"A /report/[week] page that reads aggregated usage data already being logged. Use a Canvas or html2canvas screenshot-to-PNG export for the shareable card. Hardcode 3-4 tip templates matched by simple if/else on usage patterns. Email delivery is out of scope for MVP — just an in-app banner + manual 'Download My Report Card' button. No new data pipeline needed if usage logs exist.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Report Card (9:16 aspect ratio, share-optimised): Gradient card with user's avatar initial, week label, big ₹ total spend number, 3 stat pills (top model / prompts sent / tokens used), a mini bar chart of daily spend, the personalised saving tip in a highlighted box, and AI Bazaar logo + QR code at the bottom","Screen 2 — Report Hub Page: Timeline of past weekly cards in a vertical scroll, each as a thumbnail preview with ₹ total and a 'Share' button, plus a top banner showing streak ('4 weeks on AI Bazaar 🔥') to encourage retention"],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":null}],"ideationPlan":"Chairman synthesis failed. Top ideas selected by score.","gitAnalysis":{"repoName":"AI-Bazaar","description":"AI Bazaar is an India-first AI marketplace and inference platform.","techStack":["Next.js","React","Tailwind CSS","TypeScript"],"recentCommits":["bbe7d09 fix: prevent API_KEY_HASH_SALT throw during Vercel build-time page collection","5e2a81a chore: final supervision cycle — 88/88 tasks DONE, all tests passing","f2119f2 fix: resolve Vercel build failure — API_KEY_HASH_SALT crash during page-data collection","afa4b68 fix: resolve Vercel build failure — API_KEY_HASH_SALT crash during page-data collection","34eb7b5 docs: architect session 3 — ARCHITECTURE.md rewrite, ADR-013, TASKS.md update","b39483c fix: allow API_KEY_HASH_SALT fallback during Next.js build phase","b24f986 fix: make hashSalt lazy to fix Vercel build crash","433b025 fix: make hashSalt lazy to fix Vercel build crash","c104300 chore: final supervision cycle — 88/88 tasks DONE, 93 test files (1347 tests) passing, zero TS errors","8a6636a feat: AI Bazaar v1 complete — 88/88 tasks done, 93 test files, 1347 tests passing","c8339c2 feat: AI Bazaar project 100% complete — 88/88 tasks done, 1347 tests passing","f0ae4a9 chore: supervision cycle — project 100% complete, 88/88 tasks DONE, 1347 tests passing","4413e5b feat: AI Bazaar complete — 88/88 tasks done, 1347 tests passing","3d1f935 chore: supervision cycle — 88/88 DONE, 1347/1347 tests pass, build fully clean","d0d0f2b chore: supervision cycle — all 88 tasks DONE, 1347 tests passing, build clean","1bd29e8 chore: supervision cycle — 88/88 DONE, 1347 tests pass, all agents completed, project 100% complete","7f116ce feat: AI Bazaar 100% complete — 88/88 tasks done, 1347 tests passing","2b5fc6f chore: supervision cycle — 88/88 DONE, 93 test files, 1347 tests passing, 0 lint errors, 0 type errors","f59a739 feat: AI Bazaar v1 complete — 88/88 tasks done, 1347 tests passing","d554772 feat: AI Bazaar v1.0 — all 88 tasks complete, 1347 tests passing"],"fileStructure":"./.vercel/project.json

{"selectedIdeas":[{"title":"Escrow Pulse — Real-Time Marketplace Health Dashboard","description":"A public-facing, live dashboard that visualizes the marketplace's escrow activity, dispute rates, task throughput, and policy enforcement events in real time. Think of it as a 'glass box' into the platform's integrity — showing aggregate funds locked in escrow, tasks in flight, dispute resolution timelines, and security policy triggers (from the OPA/Rego layer). Operators get operational visibility; users get trust signals before committing funds. The more transparent the system looks, the more bots and task-posters want to participate.","viralPotential":"Publicly shareable 'marketplace health' snapshot URLs (e.g., /pulse/2026-W10) let power users tweet about platform activity. Rival marketplace operators will benchmark against it. Security researchers will cite the dispute/fraud rate as a trust signal, driving organic backlinks and credibility.","mvpScope":"Wire a read-only '/api/pulse' endpoint that aggregates existing DB tables (escrow ledger, task state machine transitions, dispute records) into a JSON snapshot — no new schema needed. Build a single-page React dashboard polling every 5s with four stat cards (Escrow Locked, Tasks Active, Disputes Open, Policy Blocks Today) plus a sparkline for 24h task completion rate. Reuse the existing authenticated WebSocket endpoint for live push. Deployable in 1 week.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Live Stats Bar: Four large metric cards at the top (total escrow locked in $, active tasks, open disputes, OPA policy enforcement blocks in last 24h) with green/amber/red health indicators and delta-from-yesterday badges.","Screen 2 — Activity Sparklines: A 24-hour rolling chart of task completions vs. disputes filed, with a toggleable 'security events' overlay showing timing-safe lease expirations and CORS violation spikes — giving power users a forensic view of platform integrity."],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":null},{"title":"Task DNA — Shareable Verified Task Receipts","description":"Every completed (or disputed) task gets a cryptographically-signed, human-readable 'Task Receipt' — a permanent, shareable page that shows the task spec, the bot that claimed it, evidence submitted, escrow settlement amount, dispute outcome (if any), and the policy rules that governed it. Think of it as a blockchain explorer but for marketplace task execution. Task posters can share receipts to prove work was done; bots can link receipts as portfolio proof; researchers can cite them. It turns every task into a public artifact of verified work.","viralPotential":"Bots will share receipt URLs in their profiles and READMEs as proof of earnings and reliability — free distribution. Task posters will share receipts in forums to prove vendor accountability. The 'dispute outcome' pages will be especially shareable when a bot wins or loses a contested claim, driving discussion in automation communities.","mvpScope":"Add a '/tasks/:id/receipt' GET endpoint that assembles existing task row + escrow ledger entries + evidence metadata + dispute record into a signed JSON payload (HMAC with server key, no blockchain needed). Render it as a static-looking HTML page with a 'copy link' button and an 'export PDF' option via browser print. Dispute outcome section renders only if a dispute record exists. No new DB tables — pure read from existing schema. Shippable in 1 week.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Receipt Header: Task title, unique receipt ID, creation/completion timestamps, escrow amount locked and released, bot identifier (masked or public per privacy setting), and a green 'VERIFIED' or amber 'DISPUTED → RESOLVED' status badge with the governing policy version cited.","Screen 2 — Evidence & Policy Trail: A collapsible timeline showing each state machine transition (claimed → evidence_submitted → settled), evidence file hashes (not contents), the specific OPA policy rules evaluated, and the final arbiter decision with rationale — giving full auditability without exposing sensitive data."],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":null},{"title":"Bot Bounty Board — Community-Curated Task Request Feed","description":"A lightweight upvote-driven feed where anyone (no account required) can submit a task type they wish existed on the marketplace — e.g., 'I need a bot that monitors SEC filings and alerts on keyword matches.' The community upvotes requests; when a request crosses a threshold, it becomes a 'Bounty Brief' that verified bot developers can claim and build against, with an optional soft-pledge of escrow intent from the requester. It creates a demand-signal flywheel: task posters discover what bots are possible, bot developers build what the market actually wants.","viralPotential":"Requesters share their submission links to rally upvotes from their networks — instant distribution. Bot developers monitor the feed like a Product Hunt for automation tasks, driving developer community engagement. 'Top Bounties This Week' is a natural newsletter/tweet format that the platform can publish as organic content marketing.","mvpScope":"Three DB tables: bounty_requests (title, description, submitter_email optional, upvote_count), bounty_votes (ip-rate-limited, no auth required), bounty_claims (bot_developer_id, status). Single-page feed UI sorted by votes with a submission modal and a 'I'd fund this task' pledge field (just a number, no payment collected yet — sets expectations). Admin threshold config to auto-promote to 'Bounty Brief' status. No escrow integration in MVP — that's v2. Shippable in 1–2 weeks.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Bounty Feed: Card-based list sorted by upvotes with task category tags (data-extraction, monitoring, form-automation), a vote button (arrow + count), soft-pledge total shown as '$X in stated interest', and a 'Claim to Build' CTA visible only to authenticated bot developers — plus a floating 'Submit a Request' button.","Screen 2 — Bounty Detail & Submission Modal: Full request description, comment thread for clarifying questions, pledge leaderboard showing which task posters have expressed funding intent, and a developer claim panel showing who has claimed it and their estimated delivery timeline — creating accountability before a single line of bot code is written."],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":null}],"ideationPlan":"Chairman synthesis failed. Top ideas selected by score.","gitAnalysis":{"repoName":"Clawbot-marketplace","description":"Production-oriented implementation scaffold for a public verified Clawbot task marketplace with:","techStack":["TypeScript"],"recentCommits":["e9f0059 fix(security+hardening): BUG-HIGH-001 CORS, BUG-MED-001 timing-safe lease, BUG-MED-002 evidence filtering, BUG-HIGH-003 dispute target validation, BUG-MED-003 policy enforcement — 314 tests passing","47972df fix(api): authenticate WebSocket endpoints (BUG-CRIT-001)","a227347 feat(security+integrity): TASK-HARD-007/008/010 + TASK-FEAT-001/006 + fix pre-existing test failures","c5e9fea arch: comprehensive bug inventory — 14 untracked bugs + fix task definitions","bc3fb14 test(api): comprehensive endpoint and integration test coverage","4e16581 arch: comprehensive architecture deep-dive — Moltbook, escrow, state machines, security","7d4654b test(escrow): add comprehensive escrow and ledger test coverage (TASK-HARD-006)","00a1ba0 init: clawbot marketplace v1 alpha baseline"],"fileStructure":"./infra/deployments.log

The community upvotes requests; when a request crosses a threshold, it becomes a 'Bounty Brief' that verified bot developers can claim and build against, with an optional soft-pledge of escrow intent from the requester. It creates a demand-signal flywheel: task posters discover what bots are possible, bot developers build what the market actually wants.","viralPotential":"Requesters share their submission links to rally upvotes from their networks — instant distribution. Bot developers monitor the feed like a Product Hunt for automation tasks, driving developer community engagement. 'Top Bounties This Week' is a natural newsletter/tweet format that the platform can publish as organic content marketing.","mvpScope":"Three DB tables: bounty_requests (title, description, submitter_email optional, upvote_count), bounty_votes (ip-rate-limited, no auth required), bounty_claims (bot_developer_id, status). Single-page feed UI sorted by votes with a submission modal and a 'I'd fund this task' pledge field (just a number, no payment collected yet — sets expectations). Admin threshold config to auto-promote to 'Bounty Brief' status. No escrow integration in MVP — that's v2. Shippable in 1–2 weeks.","uiScreens":["Screen 1 — Bounty Feed: Card-based list sorted by upvotes with task category tags (data-extraction, monitoring, form-automation), a vote button (arrow + count), soft-pledge total shown as '$X in stated interest', and a 'Claim to Build' CTA visible only to authenticated bot developers — plus a floating 'Submit a Request' button.","Screen 2 — Bounty Detail & Submission Modal: Full request description, comment thread for clarifying questions, pledge leaderboard showing which task posters have expressed funding intent, and a developer claim panel showing who has claimed it and their estimated delivery timeline — creating accountability before a single line of bot code is written."],"proposedBy":"Claude Sonnet","averageScore":null}],"ideationPlan":"Chairman synthesis failed.
